Meet our leadership

Joseph Chalom

Chief Executive Officer & Board Member
In July 2025, Joseph Chalom joined Sharplink as Co-Chief Executive Officer, bringing with him over 20 years of institutional leadership at the intersection of traditional finance and digital assets. In November, he transitioned into the role of sole Chief Executive Officer, was elected to the Board and appointed to serve as a member of the Investment and Technology Committee. As former Managing Director and Head of Strategic Ecosystem Partnerships at BlackRock, Joseph was instrumental in driving the firm’s global strategy across digital assets, fintech innovation, and blockchain infrastructure. Under his leadership, BlackRock launched some of the most successful digital asset products in history, including the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), which became the largest Bitcoin ETF globally, and the iShares Ethereum Trust (ETHA), the leading spot Ethereum ETF in the U.S. He also played a key role in advancing tokenization through the launch of BUIDL — the first tokenized U.S. Treasury fund built on Ethereum — and oversaw strategic partnerships with Coinbase, Securitize, and other major players shaping the digital asset ecosystem. Joseph’s work bridged the worlds of Wall Street and digital assets, bringing compliant, institutional-grade access to crypto and helping scale blockchain-based financial infrastructure. Prior to leading BlackRock’s digital asset efforts, Joseph served as the firm’s Deputy Chief Operating Officer and Chief Operating Officer of BlackRock Solutions, where he managed the global expansion of Aladdin, the firm’s industry-leading portfolio and risk management platform. A graduate of Johns Hopkins University (BA, International Affairs) and Columbia Law School (JD), Joseph began his career as a corporate and regulatory attorney before transitioning into financial technology leadership. He also served on the boards of Securitize and Clarity AI, furthering his commitment to next-generation capital markets and sustainability-focused innovation. At SharpLink, Joseph is focused on building the most trusted Ethereum treasury and advancing the company’s mission to bring ETH to the center of global finance.
Meet our leadership

Robert DeLucia, CPA

Chief Financial Officer
Bob has over 30 years of experience and has assisted companies in various unique and complex issues dealing with technical US GAAP accounting, SEC matters and filings, and mergers and acquisitions. Prior to being recruited to serve as Sharplink’s Chief Financial Officer in August 2022, Bob served in many positions where he was tasked with leading a company’s turnaround and financial restatements. From February through December 2022, he was Chief Accounting Officer at GTT Communications, a tier one Internet backbone service supplier where he developed the plan of the accounting & finance turnaround. Prior to GTT, Bob was recruited to Adelphia Communications Corporation, Inc. after the discovery of massive accounting fraud and filing of bankruptcy in June 2002. As Adelphia’s Corporate Controller, he was the highest-ranking finance leader on-site in Coudersport, Pennsylvania. He led a five-year restatement of the accounting records and re-addressed all accounting technical issues during that time. This resulted in the restatement of five years of previously issued financial statements to the SEC. During this time, he also led a project for the Department of Justice to prepare reports demonstrating how the fraud was committed and these reports were used during the Adelphia trial concluding with three previous executive management members being convicted. Bob graduated from Robert Morris University, where he earned both Bachelor of Arts and Bachelor of Science degrees, Bob was as an Audit Senior Manager at KPMG and Arthur Anderson from June 1985 through September 1998.
Meet our leadership

Matthew Sheffield

Chief Investment Officer
Matthew brings extensive institutional trading and digital asset investment experience to Sharplink. Prior to joining our Company in October 2025, he served as Head of U.S. Spot Trading at FalconX, a leading institutional digital asset prime brokerage and trading firm. In that capacity, he led a team of traders running one of the largest OTC desks in the industry, played a key role deploying the firm into digital asset treasury investments and started their digital asset treasury strategic advisory initiative. Earlier in his career, Matthew worked at Bridgewater Associates, one of the world’s largest and most respected institutional asset managers, where he contributed to the development, improvement and implementation of systematic trading strategies across global rates and credit markets. At SharpLink, Matthew leads the firm’s capital markets strategy, capital deployment, yield optimization and strategic investment initiative aimed at enhancing ETH concentration and maximizing ETH’s treasury performance.
Meet our leadership

Michael Camarda

Chief Development Officer
Michael previously joined Sharplink from Consensys, one of the world’s leading Ethereum software companies and Sharplink’s valued strategic partner, where he led corporate development initiatives across capital raising, mergers and acquisitions and venture capital investments. Earlier in his career, Michael worked at J.P. Morgan, where he held positions in both the Investment Banking Division and the firm’s principal investments group. There, he advised clients on capital raises and M&A transactions and executed venture capital and growth equity investments on behalf of the bank. This blend of institutional finance and onchain expertise uniquely positions Michael to help bridge the gap between traditional capital markets and decentralized finance. At Sharplink, Michael is responsible for building and scaling capital markets and corporate development strategy, including the design and execution of innovative financing structures, tokenization initiatives, and institutional partnerships.
Meet our leadership

Mandy Campbell

Chief Marketing Officer
In October 2025, Mandy joined Sharplink from Bain Capital Crypto, where she led marketing for their dedicated digital asset and early-stage crypto venture funds. Prior to Bain Capital, Mandy led content and brand for OKX, where she drove growth of the global crypto exchange during a period of accelerated expansion. Earlier roles include strategic positions at GitHub, Lyft and Facebook, where she scaled creative and content functions, and helped forge brand identity. At Sharplink, Mandy leads the Company’s global brand strategy, marketing and communications efforts across institutional, retail and crypto-native audiences.
Meet our leadership

Dodi Handy

Vice President, Business and Legal Affairs

Prior to her appointment as Vice President of Business and Legal Affairs in October 2025, Dodi served as Sharplink’s Director of Communications since May 2021, and was later named Vice President of Corporate Communications on June 1, 2025 and then Vice President of Business and Legal Affairs in October 2025.
 
Broadly recognized as one of Wall Street’s most experienced and trusted corporate communications executives, she has devoted the past 30+ years crafting comprehensive public and investor relations platforms for her valued private, pre-public and publicly traded clients through her specialty consulting practice, WallStreetWriter, LLC, a firm she founded in November 2014.  Prior to founding WallStreetWriter, Dodi served as President, CEO and Managing Principal of Elite Financial Communications Group, LLC, an award-winning IR/PR firm she founded in 2001, which ultimately merged with a leading capital access advisory firm, Hanover International, Inc., in November 2014.
 
Prior to Elite, Dodi rose through the ranks at Madison & Wall Worldwide, Inc., beginning first in 1993 as Vice President of Operations and advancing over an eight-year period to become the firm’s President and CEO. Previously, she served as President and CEO of Advantage List & Marketing, a direct response and list management agency, as well as General Sales Manager of Direct Response Marketing, Inc. and later DataBase Direct, two of the largest direct marketing and lettershop firms in the Southeast. In these capacities, she was responsible for managing direct marketing needs of national clientele that included the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee in Washington, D.C., TimeWarner Cable, Universal Studios-Florida and Premier Cruise Lines.
 
Dodi began her professional career serving a four-year tour of duty with the U.S. Army, where she was recognized as the “Soldier of the Mount” by Fort Dix, New Jersey and “Soldier of the Post” by Fort Jackson, South Carolina, as well as “Soldier of the Year” by both VCorps and US Army/Europe. She is the proud recipient of numerous military awards, including three Army Achievement Medals and the Army Commendation Medal.

Meet our leadership

Steven Ehrlich

Head of Research and Communications

Steven is Head of Research and Communications at Sharplink. He previously served as Director of Research for Digital Assets at Forbes. Prior to this, he was at Kraken, a cryptocurrency exchange based in the U.S. Before joining Kraken, he served as Chief Operating Officer at the Wall Street Blockchain Alliance, a non-profit trade association dedicated to the comprehensive adoption of cryptocurrencies and blockchain technologies across global markets. Before joining the WSBA, he was the Lead Associate within the Emerging Technologies practice at Spitzberg Partners, a boutique corporate advisory firm that advises leading firms across industries on blockchain technology. Previously, he was Vice President/Lead Strategy Analyst at Citi FinTech, where he drove strategic and new business development initiatives for Citigroup's Global Retail and Consumer Bank business across 20 countries.

He also served five years as a Senior Intelligence Analyst at Booz Allen Hamilton supporting the U.S. Department of Defense. He is the host of Bits + Bips: The Interview, a weekly show on the Unchained on Air network, and he has a B.S. in Business Administration from the Tepper School of Business at Carnegie Mellon University and an M.A. in International Affairs from Columbia University's School of International and Public Affairs.

Meet our leadership

Tori Roberts

VP, Affiliate Marketing

Tori joined Sharplink's leadership team as Vice President, Affiliate Marketing on December 31, 2021 in tandem with the closing of the Company’s acquisition of FourCubed’s iGaming and affiliate marketing network focused on delivering quality traffic and player acquisitions, retention ad conversions to iGaming operator partners worldwide.  She was subsequently named as Vice President, Marketing in June 2025.

Previously, Tori served as President of FourCubed beginning in 2014, where she managed the Company’s award winning affiliate/direct marketing, acquisitions, retention and performance efforts for its network of over 12,000 affiliates and 1.8 million referred players since the inception of the network.  

During her tenure as President, FourCubed’s PAS group was recognized as the European online gambling industry’s Top Affiliate Manager, Top Affiliate Website and Top Affiliate Program for four consecutive years by both igamingbusiness.com and igamingaffiliate.com. 

In 2006, she began her career at AT&T Mobility, serving as a National Account Executive responsible for high profile corporate accounts.  She honed her B2B and B2C sales, marketing and leadership skills over the next eight years at Verizon Wireless, Clearwire, Best Buy, Scout Media and DreamPlanGo.  Tori earned an Associate of Applied Science degree in Business Administration and a Bachelor of Science degree in Business Management from the Minnesota School of Business.

Meet our leadership

Danny Scalone

Chief of Staff

At Sharplink, Danny serves as Chief of Staff, operating at the intersection of strategy, execution, and operations. He brings over nine years of experience in People Operations and Human Resources, with deep expertise across employee relations, performance management, organizational design, HRIS, and compensation. His career has been defined by building scalable people infrastructure that supports growth, accountability, and strong culture as organizations evolve.

He partners closely with executive leadership to drive cross-functional alignment and translate strategic priorities into measurable outcomes, with a focus on the systems, processes, and people infrastructure that allow Sharplink to scale.

Meet our leadership

Dana Perez

Chief Accounting Officer
Dana brings over 25 years of specialized accounting and financial leadership experience to Sharplink, with expertise spanning SEC compliance and reporting, technical US GAAP accounting, audit management, and financial statement preparation. Dana first engaged with Sharplink in 2022 as a financial consultant, and her impact on the Company’s financial infrastructure led to her appointment as Senior Vice President of Accounting in September 2025. She was promoted to Chief Accounting Officer in March 2026. Since 2020, Dana has served as Principal of Eschenburg Perez CPA, LLC, a specialty financial consulting firm she founded to deliver fractional CFO and controller services across a broad client base. During this period, she served as Chief Financial Officer for three publicly traded companies while continuing to serve private company and non-profit clients — a testament to her ability to manage complex, multi-entity financial environments at scale. Prior to launching her firm, Dana served for eight years as Chief Financial Officer of Adopt-A-Family of the Palm Beaches, Inc. Earlier in her career, she held the roles of Manager of the National Office of Risk Management and Audit Manager at RSM US LLP, the fifth largest accounting firm in the United States, advising clients across commercial, government contracting, manufacturing, and software development industries. Dana holds a Master of Science and a Bachelor of Science in Accountancy, both from the University of North Carolina at Wilmington, and is a licensed CPA in the State of Florida. She is a member of the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants. At Sharplink, Dana leads the Company's accounting and financial reporting function, with direct responsibility for SOX 404 compliance buildout, audit oversight, and the integrity of Sharplink's financial accounting and reporting processes. She is focused on establishing the highest standards of internal controls and regulatory compliance, and works in close collaboration with the Company's finance and digital asset leadership to support Sharplink's Ethereum treasury strategy and its position at the forefront of digital asset finance.
Meet our board of directors

Joseph Lubin

Chairman of the Board
In May 2025, Joseph Lubin was appointed to Sharplink's Board of Directors and named our Chairman. He is a co-founder of Ethereum and the founder of Consensys, a full-stack, global blockchain company. Joseph has established himself as a guiding force in the fast-growing blockchain industry and a powerful advocate of decentralized technology. Born and raised in Toronto, Joseph graduated from Princeton University with a degree in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science. He worked in the Princeton Robotics Lab at Tomandandy Music developing an autonomous music composition tool and at private research firm Vision Applications Inc. building autonomous mobile robots. As a software engineer and consultant, Joseph worked with eMagine on the Identrus cryptographic payments and trade finance network project and was involved in the founding and operation of a hedge fund. He held positions as Director of the New York office of Blacksmith Software Consulting and VP of Technology in Private Wealth Management at Goldman Sachs. Through these posts, he focused on the intersection of cryptography, engineering and finance. Inspired by Satoshi Nakamoto’s Bitcoin white paper and the potential of distributed systems to “shatter the silos of power and re-balance the information asymmetries that disadvantage so many” Joseph delved into the incipient blockchain community. He was a primary figure in the creation and launch of Ethereum alongside fellow Torontonian Vitalik Buterin in 2014, and eventually moved to build an ecosystem around the nascent technology, in part by launching Consensys.
Meet our leadership

Joseph Chalom

Chief Executive Officer & Board Member
In July 2025, Joseph Chalom joined Sharplink as Co-Chief Executive Officer, bringing with him over 20 years of institutional leadership at the intersection of traditional finance and digital assets. In November, he transitioned into the role of sole Chief Executive Officer, was elected to the Board and appointed to serve as a member of the Investment and Technology Committee. As former Managing Director and Head of Strategic Ecosystem Partnerships at BlackRock, Joseph was instrumental in driving the firm’s global strategy across digital assets, fintech innovation, and blockchain infrastructure. Under his leadership, BlackRock launched some of the most successful digital asset products in history, including the iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT), which became the largest Bitcoin ETF globally, and the iShares Ethereum Trust (ETHA), the leading spot Ethereum ETF in the U.S. He also played a key role in advancing tokenization through the launch of BUIDL — the first tokenized U.S. Treasury fund built on Ethereum — and oversaw strategic partnerships with Coinbase, Securitize, and other major players shaping the digital asset ecosystem. Joseph’s work bridged the worlds of Wall Street and digital assets, bringing compliant, institutional-grade access to crypto and helping scale blockchain-based financial infrastructure. Prior to leading BlackRock’s digital asset efforts, Joseph served as the firm’s Deputy Chief Operating Officer and Chief Operating Officer of BlackRock Solutions, where he managed the global expansion of Aladdin, the firm’s industry-leading portfolio and risk management platform. A graduate of Johns Hopkins University (BA, International Affairs) and Columbia Law School (JD), Joseph began his career as a corporate and regulatory attorney before transitioning into financial technology leadership. He also served on the boards of Securitize and Clarity AI, furthering his commitment to next-generation capital markets and sustainability-focused innovation. At SharpLink, Joseph is focused on building the most trusted Ethereum treasury and advancing the company’s mission to bring ETH to the center of global finance.
Meet our board of directors

Leslie Bernhard

Board Member
Leslie was appointed to the Board of Sharplink in February 2024 and serves as Chairman of the Audit Committee and a member of both the Compensation Committee and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committees. She has served as chairman of the board of Nasdaq-listed Nexalin Technology, Inc. since November 2023. From February 2017 through the present, Leslie has also been an independent director of Sachem Capital Corp., a NYSE American-listed REIT real estate investment trust). In addition, she has served as the non-executive chairman of the board of Milestone Scientific Inc. (‘Milestone”), an NYSE American-listed developer and manufacturer of medical and dental devices, since October 2009, and as an independent director of Milestone since May 2003. She also served as Interim Chief Executive Officer of Milestone from October 2017 to December 2017. In 2024, she was appointed to the board of The iRemedy Healthcare Companies, Inc., a privately held, global medical supply and healthcare technology company. From 2007 through September 2018, Leslie was an independent director of Universal Power Group, Inc., a global supplier of power solutions In 1986, she co-founded AdStar, Inc., an electronic ad intake service to the newspaper industry, and served as its president, chief executive officer and executive director until 2012. Earlier in her career, Leslie held management positions at Revlon, Inc., Walt Disney Productions, Inc. and the Gillette Company. She earned a Bachelor of Science Degree in Education from St. John’s University.
Meet our board of directors

Obie McKenzie

Board Member
Obie was a founding board member of the National Association of Securities Professionals, where he received the “Wall Street Hall of Fame Award” in 2001. In 2010, he was bestowed the AIMSE Richard A. Lothrop Outstanding Achievement Award in recognition for his outstanding achievements in the investment management industry and his community. In 2011, Obie was named by "Black Enterprise Magazine" as one of the "75 Most Powerful Blacks on Wall Street;" and in 2013, he was named "Public Fund Marketer of the Year" by "Money Management Intelligence", ​ In addition to serving on Sharplink's Board of Directors since his appointment in February 2024, he is also a member of the Boards of Nasdaq-listed MGO Global Inc. and privately-held iRemedy Healthcare Companies, Inc. Obie is also a member of the Board of Trustees for Tennessee State University. Obie earned a Bachelor of Science degree from Tennessee State University and an MBA from Harvard Business School. In 2017, he also received an Honorary Doctorate from the University of Hartford.
Meet our board of directors

Robert Gutkowski

Board Member
Robert was appointed to the Board of Sharplink in February 2024 and serves as a member of the Audit, Compensation and Nominating and Corporate Governance Committees. Over a career spanning more than five decades, Bob has proven to be a significant asset builder in the sports, entertainment and media industries. Among his key achievements was the landmark New York Yankees-Madison Square Garden Network $486 million cable distribution deal. This transaction forever changed the sports economic landscape, increased the value of the MSG Network by nearly 2,000%, and enabled the Yankees to dominate Major League Baseball media over the past two decades. In addition, he was the original architect behind radio entrepreneur Bob Sillerman’s roll-up of regional, independent concert promoters, which created SFX Entertainment – the first nationwide, vertically-integrated, concert promotion company widely recognized for transforming the multi-billion dollar live music concert industry. From October 2014 through the present, Bob has led RMG Sports Ventures LLC, a company he founded to originate and advise private equity and other institutional capital on investments in sports, entertainment and media. He recently co-originated the acquisition of True Temper Sports (the largest producer of golf shafts in the world) for Lincolnshire Management and made a substantial investment alongside Lincolnshire in True Temper. In December 1991, Bob was named President of Madison Square Garden, where he was responsible for the operations of the New York Knicks basketball team; the New York Rangers hockey team – which won the 1994 Stanley Cup Championship; MSG Communications – including the MSG Network, the nation’s largest regional cable network; MSG Entertainment and the MSG Facilities Group, which operated The Garden Arena and The Paramount Theater. He originally joined Madison Square Garden in 1985 and over the next six years held various senior executive positions, including President of the MSG Network. Under his leadership, the subscriber base of the MSG Network – the oldest and largest regional sports network in the country – more than doubled to 5.1 million subscribers. The Yankees, together with the New York Knicks and the New York Rangers, became the foundations of MSG Network’s year-round operation. In 1993 and 1994, the Garden was the most active building in the country in bookings and revenues and was named “Arena of the Year” by Pollstar Magazine.

Maximizing the Economic Layer of Ethereum

See Investor InformationSee Investor Information
Explore The DashboardExplore The Dashboard

Institution-Caliber Ethereum Exposure, For Everyone

THE INSIGHT
Ethereum is becoming the foundational layer of the global financial system. Stablecoins, tokenized assets, and onchain markets are increasingly settling on a network that runs continuously and globally. This isn’t a future promise. It’s already happening. As more and more financial activities, from stablecoins to tokenized assets, move onchain — the real question is no longer whether Ethereum matters, but how investors benefit from this supercycle in a way that is disciplined, transparent, and built to last.
THE BRIDGE
Sharplink answers that question. Ethereum-native assets behave differently than traditional financial instruments. They generate yield, reflect network usage, and operate within a live economic system. Public markets, by contrast, are built on institutional-standards for governance, transparency, and accountability. Sharplink bridges these two worlds by operating an ETH-first treasury and capital platform that applies institutional-grade infrastructure to an onchain asset, with a clear objective to grow ETH per share over time through active treasury management and disciplined capital markets execution.
THE EDGE
Doing this well requires more than enthusiasm for Ethereum (though we have that in spades). It demands experience, an understanding of how public companies are governed, and systems that make performance visible as it happens. Sharplink brings together Ethereum-native expertise and public-market discipline, with real-time transparency and accountability built into how the company operates at every step.

This isn’t a trade. This is something much bigger — a model for how mission-driven organizations advance shared goals of decentralization, protocol-native finance, and global economic empowerment.

Joseph LubinCo-Founder of Ethereum & Chairman of Sharplink

Sharplink is led by people who helped build the institutional Ethereum ecosystem itself. Our focus is productivity, stewardship, discipline, and durability.
